title: `--cgroups=split` does not work with customized cpuset
body:
### Issue Description

Containers with `--cgroups=split` fail to start with customized cpuset created by [cpuset](https://github.com/SUSE/cpuset). This also affects containers created by Quadlet.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```console
$ # mount cpuset
$ sudo mkdir /dev/cpuset
$ sudo mount -t cpuset cpuset /dev/cpuset

$ # shield any cpu using cset
$ sudo cset shield --cpu 0-5
cset: --> activating shielding:
cset: moving 1418 tasks from root into system cpuset...
[==================================================]%
cset: "system" cpuset of CPUSPEC(6-13) with 1418 tasks running
cset: "user" cpuset of CPUSPEC(0-5) with 0 tasks running
```

### Describe the results you received

```console
$ # containers without --cgroups=split work well
$ podman run -it --rm alpine
/ # 

$ # containers with --cgroups=split fail to start
$ podman run --cgroups=split -it --rm alpine
Error: mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/cpuset: permission denied
```

### Describe the results you expected

Containers with `--cgroups=split` can successfully start.

comments.body:
`--cgroups=split` uses the current cgroup.

Are you already able to write to it?

> `--cgroups=split` uses the current cgroup.
> 
> Are you already able to write to it?

It's writable.

```console
$ cat /proc/self/cgroup
1:cpuset:/system
0::/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/app.slice/app-org.gnome.Terminal.slice/vte-spawn-c06a9257-e2de-45d4-8118-431232e12b0c.scope/runtime
$ ls -lha /sys/fs/cgroup/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/app.slice/app-org.gnome.Terminal.slice/vte-spawn-c06a9257-e2de-45d4-8118-431232e12b0c.scope/runtime
total 0
drwxr-xr-x 2 msk msk 0 Nov 19 07:14 .
drwxr-xr-x 3 msk msk 0 Nov 19 07:14 ..
-r--r--r-- 1 msk msk 0 Nov 19 07:14 cgroup.controllers
-r--r--r-- 1 msk msk 0 Nov 19 07:14 cgroup.events
-rw-r--r-- 1 msk msk 0 Nov 19 07:14 cgroup.freeze
--w------- 1 msk msk 0 Nov 19 07:14 cgroup.kill

-rw-r--r-- 1 msk msk 0 Nov 19 07:14 cpu.pressure

-r--r--r-- 1 msk msk 0 Nov 19 07:14 cpu.stat.local
-rw-r--r-- 1 msk msk 0 Nov 19 07:14 cpu.uclamp.max
-rw-r--r-- 1 msk msk 0 Nov 19 07:14 cpu.uclamp.min
-rw-r--r-- 1 msk msk 0 Nov 19 07:14 cpu.weight

-r--r--r-- 1 msk msk 0 Nov 19 07:14 memory.events

-rw-r--r-- 1 msk msk 0 Nov 19 07:14 memory.max
-rw-r--r-- 1 msk msk 0 Nov 19 07:14 memory.min
-r--r--r-- 1 msk msk 0 Nov 19 07:14 memory.numa_stat
-rw-r--r-- 1 msk msk 0 Nov 19 07:14 memory.oom.group

-r--r--r-- 1 msk msk 0 Nov 19 07:14 memory.stat
-r--r--r-- 1 msk msk 0 Nov 19 07:14 memory.swap.current

-r--r--r-- 1 msk msk 0 Nov 19 07:14 memory.zswap.current
-rw-r--r-- 1 msk msk 0 Nov 19 07:14 memory.zswap.max

$ cat /sys/fs/cgroup/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/app.slice/app-org.gnome.Terminal.slice/vte-spawn-c06a9257-e2de-45d4-8118-431232e12b0c.scope/runtime/cgroup.controllers
cpu io memory pids
$ podman run --cgroups=split -it --rm alpine
Error: mkdir /sys/fs/cgroup/cpuset: permission denied
$ ls /sys/fs/cgroup/cpuset
ls: cannot access '/sys/fs/cgroup/cpuset': No such file or directory
```

podman/crun do not support that cgroup configuration where some controllers are on cgroup v1 and some others on cgroup v2.

Either use cgroup v1 or cgroup v2 (in this case you just need to enable the cpuset controller).
